title: Unicode Overview
subtitle: Current position and why the issue keeps returning
summary: Short baseline explanation of why official Unicode SignWriting is not sufficient for the Sutton SignWriting production ecosystem.
synopsis: This overview states the current position plainly. The official Unicode SignWriting encoding covers a symbol inventory, but it does not provide a workable compatible model for written signs in practice. The real issues include spatial composition, sorting, tooling, dataset compatibility, and the difference between encoded symbols and usable writing.

slides.title: The Baseline Problem
slides.summary: The current official Unicode SignWriting encoding is not compatible with the established Sutton SignWriting production ecosystem.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for Unicode overview baseline.
bullets: Sorting, tooling, and dataset compatibility still matter
FSW and SWU remain the practical working encodings
slides.speech: The baseline issue is not whether Unicode contains SignWriting symbols. It does. The issue is whether the current official Unicode SignWriting design can carry the established Sutton SignWriting production ecosystem without loss, redesign, or incompatible workarounds. The answer today is no. The unresolved problems include spatial composition, sorting, tooling, and compatibility with real data and production workflows.
slides.notes: Use this as the shortest stable explanation.
